name: barge-vscode
displayName: bARGE - boosted Azure Resource Graph Explorer
description: bARGE is a boosted Azure Resource Graph Explorer for Visual Studio Code, with features for comparison and insights into KQL results.

commandPalette.title: Run Selected Query
commandPalette.when: (editorHasSelection || barge.hasImplicitQuery) && (editorLangId == kql || resourceExtname == .kql || editorLangId == powershell || resourceExtname == .ps1 || editorLangId == plaintext)

editor/context.title: bARGE: Run Query from File (New Tab)
bARGE: Run Selected Query
editor/context.when: (editorHasSelection || barge.hasImplicitQuery) && (editorLangId == kql || resourceExtname == .kql || editorLangId == powershell || resourceExtname == .ps1 || editorLangId == plaintext)

editor/context.title: bARGE: Run Selected Query (New Tab)
editor/context.when: (editorHasSelection || barge.hasImplicitQuery) && (editorLangId == kql || resourceExtname == .kql || editorLangId == powershell || resourceExtname == .ps1 || editorLangId == plaintext)

barge.autoAuthenticate.description: Automatically authenticate to Azure using DefaultAzureCredential on extension activation

barge.hideLoginMessages.description: Hide login notifications and messages, except errors.

barge.enableHoverTooltips.description: Enable IntelliSense hover tooltips for KQL syntax.

barge.enableCompletions.description: Enable IntelliSense completions for KQL syntax.

barge.enableRunQueryCodeLens.description: Show 'Run Query' buttons above query blocks in KQL files.

barge.queryPageSize.markdownDescription:
Number of records to fetch per Azure Resource Graph API call.

*Warning: Low values may result in rate limiting.*

languageModelTools.name: barge_list_tables
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns a list of all bARGE result panels currently open in VS Code. Each entry describes one result table: its unique tableId, the source KQL file it belongs to, whether it is the currently selected table (isCurrentTarget=true — the one marked with the blue dot, i.e. the panel that the next 'Run Query' action would target), and metadata about its last query result (columns, row count, timestamp, etc.). Use this tool to discover which tables are available before calling barge_get_table_data.

languageModelTools.name: barge_get_table_data
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the full query result data for a specific bARGE result panel identified by tableId (obtained from barge_list_tables). The response includes column definitions and data rows. Use the optional maxRows parameter to limit the number of rows returned; the response will indicate whether the result was truncated.

tableId.description: The unique identifier of the result table to retrieve, in the format '<sourceFile>:<creationOrder>' (e.g. 'storage.kql:1'). Obtain valid tableIds from the barge_list_tables tool.

maxRows.description: Maximum number of data rows to return. Omit or set to 0 to return all rows. Use this to avoid very large responses when working with large result sets.

languageModelTools.name: barge_select_rows
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Selects (highlights) specific rows in a bARGE result table. The selected rows are visually highlighted in the webview and opened in the detail/comparison panel. Use 0-based row indices from the data returned by barge_get_table_data. This is useful for drawing the user's attention to specific rows, e.g. outliers or rows matching certain criteria.

tableId.description: The unique identifier of the result table (e.g. 'storage.kql:1'). Obtain valid tableIds from barge_list_tables.

rowIndices.description: Array of 0-based row indices to select. The rows will be highlighted and shown in the comparison/detail pane.

languageModelTools.name: barge_select_cells
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Selects (highlights) specific individual cells in a bARGE result table. Each cell is identified by a 0-based row and column index. Use this to pinpoint exact values of interest, e.g. highlighting a cell that contains an unusual SKU or a specific property value. The cells are visually highlighted in the table.

tableId.description: The unique identifier of the result table (e.g. 'storage.kql:1'). Obtain valid tableIds from barge_list_tables.

cells.description: Array of cells to select, each with a row and column index (0-based).

languageModelTools.name: barge_run_query
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Executes a KQL query against Azure Resource Graph and displays the results in a bARGE panel. The user must be authenticated first (use the bARGE: Sign In command). Returns metadata about the result (columns, row count, execution time) — use barge_get_table_data to retrieve the actual data rows afterwards. Optionally specify an existing tableId to reuse that panel; otherwise a new panel is created.

query.description: The KQL query to execute against Azure Resource Graph (e.g. 'Resources | where type == "microsoft.compute/virtualmachines" | project name, location, resourceGroup | limit 50').

tableId.description: Optional. The tableId of an existing bARGE panel to run the query in. If omitted, a new panel is created. Obtain valid tableIds from barge_list_tables.

languageModelTools.name: barge_sort_table
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Sorts a bARGE result table by a specified column name in ascending or descending order. The sort is applied visually in the webview table. Use column names as returned by barge_list_tables or barge_get_table_data.

tableId.description: The unique identifier of the result table (e.g. 'storage.kql:1'). Obtain valid tableIds from barge_list_tables.

column.description: The name of the column to sort by (e.g. 'name', 'location'). Must match a column name from the result set.

direction.description: Sort direction: 'asc' for ascending (default) or 'desc' for descending.

languageModelTools.name: barge_filter_table
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Applies column filters to a bARGE result table to show only rows where specified columns match given values. Replaces any existing filters. Each filter specifies a column name and an array of string values to include (matching is based on the display text of each cell). Use this to narrow down results, e.g. show only resources in 'eastus' or only 'Premium' SKUs.

tableId.description: The unique identifier of the result table (e.g. 'storage.kql:1'). Obtain valid tableIds from barge_list_tables.

column.description: The name of the column to filter on (e.g. 'location', 'sku').

values.description: Array of string values to include. Only rows where the column value matches one of these values will be shown.

filters.description: Array of column filters to apply. Multiple filters are combined with AND logic (all must match).

scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
scripts.test:webview:headed: tsc -p src/test/tsconfig.webview.json && HEADED=1 mocha out/src/test/webview.interactive.test.js --ui tdd --timeout 300000
